title: cerastium arvense f. calcicola
intro: Cerastium arvense f. calcicola, also known as a calcicolous form of the field chickweed, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Cerastium arvense plant species. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense f. calcicola include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits and herbal preparations.
